parameters.jsCode:
const BASE_SPEC_PROMPT = `You are an expert steel specification parser. Return ONLY valid JSON. No commentary. Read the entire PDF and decide the matching structure yourself. Do not rely on predeclared fields such as revision, effective_date, requires_impact, or requires_ce.

Return this JSON schema:
{
  "spec_name": "",
  "spec_code": "",
  "spec_title": "",
  "display_name": "",
  "customer": "",
  "notes": "",
  "branches": [
    {
      "branch_key": "",
      "name": "",
      "selector_summary": "",
      "spec_type": "",
      "astm_equivalent": [],
      "grades": [],
      "manufacturer_grades": [],
      "approved_substitutes": [],
      "ai_summary": "",
      "selector": {
        "field": "",
        "operator": "",
        "value": null,
        "unit": ""
      },
      "product_forms": [
        {
          "form": "",
          "thickness_min": null,
          "thickness_max": null,
          "thickness_unit": "",
          "description": ""
        }
      ],
      "chem_limits": [
        {
          "element": "",
          "min": null,
          "max": null,
          "unit": "%",
          "applies_to_forms": [],
          "applies_to_grades": [],
          "source": ""
        }
      ],
      "mech_limits": [
        {
          "property": "",
          "min": null,
          "max": null,
          "unit": "",
          "specimen_size": "",
          "direction": "",
          "applies_to_forms": [],
          "applies_to_grades": [],
          "source": ""
        }
      ],
      "impact_limits": [
        {
          "temperature": null,
          "temperature_unit": "",
          "coupon_size": "",
          "min_average": null,
          "min_individual": null,
          "unit": "",
          "min_readings": 3,
          "orientation": "",
          "applies_to_forms": [],
          "applies_to_grades": [],
          "source": ""
        }
      ],
      "condition_rules": [
        {
          "target_element": "",
          "condition_element": "",
          "condition_type": "",
          "condition_threshold": null,
          "target_adjustment": null,
          "target_new_max": null,
          "description": ""
        }
      ],
      "ce_thresholds": [
        {
          "thickness_min": null,
          "thickness_max": null,
          "thickness_unit": "",
          "max_ce": null,
          "applies_to_forms": [],
          "applies_to_grades": []
        }
      ],
      "custom_rules": [
        {
          "rule_type": "",
          "label": "",
          "field_name": "",
          "operator": "",
          "value_text": "",
          "value_number": null,
          "value_unit": "",
          "description": ""
        }
      ],
      "notes": ""
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Read the entire document and decide all matching branches yourself.
- Create one branch for every distinct matching path such as thickness ranges, form splits, grade columns, temperature tables, or specimen-size branches.
- Put branch-specific limits inside that branch.
- Use the top-level fields for identity only.
- Do not invent values.
- Use numbers for numeric values.
- When the document has thickness splits like 0.6 mm and below versus above 0.6 mm, create separate branches for each split.
- When tubing and pipe, plate and sheet, or grade columns differ, create separate branches.
- When a condition changes the limit based on chemistry, thickness, form, temperature, or orientation, model that as a separate branch or branch-scoped condition rule.
- References and qualifying specs should be stored as notes unless they are actual limits.
- If a limit is only advisory or recommended, keep it in notes unless the document clearly makes it mandatory.
- Normalize numeric values into the target units before output. For strength limits, convert yield/tensile into PSI. For hardness, use HBW (Brinell). For elongation and reduction of area, use Percentage. For thickness and plate dimensions, use Inches. For impact temperature, use Fahrenheit. For impact energy, use ft-lbf.
- For any branch, selector_summary, notes, or branch name that mentions thickness, express the thickness in inches, not millimeters. If the source text is in mm, convert it to inches and write the inches value in the output text as well.
- If you need traceability, include the original PDF unit in a source_unit field, but min/max values must already be converted to the normalized unit.
- Put anything that does not fit the core tabs into custom_rules so the branch stays flexible without new schema changes.

Return JSON only.`;

return items.map((item) => {
  const j = item.json || {};
  return {
    json: {
      ...j,
      selected_prompt: BASE_SPEC_PROMPT,
      extraction_system_message: BASE_SPEC_PROMPT,
      prompt_key: String(j.spec_customer_key || "UNKNOWN").trim().toUpperCase()
    },
    binary: item.binary
  };
});
